zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh le Despenser於大約1140年出生在英國盧特藍德格拉爾時，他的父親Thurstan le Despenser19歲，母親Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire18歲。他於1219年之前與Mary de Quincy結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1226年在英國英格蘭波克夏被列為high sheriff of berkshire。他去世於1238年2月23日在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾艾維沙安享年99歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Hugh le Despenser于大约1140年出生在英国盧特藍德格拉爾时，他的父亲Thurstan le Despenser19岁，母亲Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire18岁。他于1219年之前与Mary de Quincy结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1226年在英国英格兰波克夏被列为high sheriff of berkshire。他去世于1238年2月23日在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾艾維沙安享年99岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh le Despenser was born about 1140, in Ryhall, Rutland, England, his father, Thurstan le Despenser, was 19 and his mother, Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, was 18. He married Mary de Quincy before 1219. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. In 1226, at the age of 87, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of berkshire in Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 23 February 1238, in Evesham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 99, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh le Despenser est né environ 1140, à Ryhall, Rutland, Angleterre, son père, Thurstan le Despenser, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Mary de Quincy avant 1219. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. En 1226, à l’âge de 87 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of berkshire à Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 23 février 1238, à Evesham, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 99 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Hugh le Despenser um 1140 in Ryhall, Rutland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thurstan le Despenser 19 und seine Mutter Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire 18. Er hat vor 1219 Mary de Quincy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1226, als er 87 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als high sheriff of berkshire in Berkshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 23. Februar 1238 im Alter von 99 Jahren in Evesham, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Despenser è nato il circa 1140 nel Ryhall, Rutland, England, il suo padre, Thurstan le Despenser, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Mary de Quincy prima del 1219. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1226, all'età di 87 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of berkshire nel Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 23 febbraio 1238, nel Evesham, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 99 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Despenserが約1140年にイギリスルトランドリハルで生まれた時，父Thurstan le Despenserは19歳，母Lady Lucia de Oxfordshireは18歳であった。1219年以前にMary de Quincyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1226年，87歳の時，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアでの職業はhigh sheriff of berkshireと記録されている。彼は1238年2月23日にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエベシャムで99歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh le Despenser(이)가 대략 1140년 영국 루틀랜드 리홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thurstan le Despenser(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1219년 이전 Mary de Quincy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1226년, 87세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르에서 high sheriff of berkshire이었다. 그는 1238년 2월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 에베샤에서 99세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh le Despenser nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thurstan le Despenser, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary de Quincy antes 1219. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1226, com 87 anos, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of berkshire em Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 23 de fevereiro de 1238, em Evesham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 99 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh le Despenser родился около 1140 г. в местности рихолл, рутланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Thurstan le Despenser было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary de Quincy перед 1219 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1226 году, в возрасте 87 лет,  он работал по профессии high sheriff of berkshire в местности биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 23 февраля 1238 г. в местности эвешам, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 99 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Hugh le Despenser nació aproximadamente 1140, en Ryhall, Rutland, Inglaterra, su papá, Thurstan le Despenser, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Lady Lucia de Oxfordshire, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Mary de Quincy antes de 1219. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1226, a la edad de 87 años, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of berkshire en Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 23 de febrero de 1238, en Evesham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 99 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
